title: rc6 gate reports PASS while running only a CLI hook test subset
description: The root rc6:gate script is the named release/completion gate, but scripts/rc6-coverage-gate.mjs intentionally targets only four CLI hook test files instead of the full @fenglimg/fabric-cli test suite, while still running server/shared tests and producing a PASS result. docs/TESTING.md lists the CLI surface snapshot as a drift gate, and the CLI test tree contains many non-hook suites, so CLI command/help/install/i18n drift can regress while rc6:gate remains green. This is stronger than the documented partial CLI surface limitation because the release gate omits that drift gate entirely.
fix_direction: Make rc6:gate run the full @fenglimg/fabric-cli test suite or an explicit maintained CLI release-suite that includes cli-surface.test.ts and other public CLI drift/contract tests; if a subset is unavoidable, change the output/name to say it is a hook-only gate and add a separate required CLI gate in package.json/CI.

context.notes: Discovered by comprehensive testing issue discovery DBP-20260609-013801

issue_history.note: Discovered by comprehensive testing issue discovery DBP-20260609-013801

title: fabric store switch-write lacks end-to-end coverage for resolved-bindings snapshot refresh
description: The public onboarding flow tells users to run `fabric store bind` and then `fabric store switch-write`, and hooks read the resolved-bindings snapshot for read/write labels. `bind` refreshes that snapshot, but the `switch-write` command only calls `storeSwitchWrite` and prints success; the helper test asserts only active/default write config fields. A regression can leave the snapshot write_target stale after changing the write store while tests pass.
fix_direction: Add a command-level integration test that seeds two mounted writable stores, runs the public `store switch-write` command, reads the resolved-bindings snapshot, and asserts `write_target.alias` changes. Update the command to call `regenerateBindingsSnapshot(projectRoot, ...)` after `storeSwitchWrite`; consider the same assertion for `route-write` when write routes affect snapshot resolution.

context.notes: Discovered by comprehensive testing issue discovery DBP-20260609-013801

issue_history.note: Discovered by comprehensive testing issue discovery DBP-20260609-013801

title: doctor mutation commands lack real CLI-to-server filesystem integration coverage
description: The public `fabric doctor --fix` and `fabric doctor --fix-knowledge` branches call server mutation APIs, render summaries, set exit codes, and run extra CLI-side repairs, but CLI tests mock `@fenglimg/fabric-server` throughout while server tests call `runDoctorFix`/`runDoctorApplyLint` directly. This leaves no gate that runs the actual doctor command against a real temp project/store and verifies both filesystem mutation and CLI output/exit-code behavior across package boundaries.
fix_direction: Add one or two slower integration tests that import/run `doctorCommand` without mocking `@fenglimg/fabric-server`, seed a temp project/global store state with a deterministic fixable condition, invoke `--fix` or `--fix-knowledge --yes`, then assert on-disk changes, JSON/human output, store diagnostics, alias-link side effects, and `process.exitCode`.

context.notes: Discovered by comprehensive testing issue discovery DBP-20260609-013801

issue_history.note: Discovered by comprehensive testing issue discovery DBP-20260609-013801

title: Events JSONL G11 invariant test still asserts rc37 metric-counter overlap
description: events-jsonl-gates.ts documents G11 as the structural invariant that every METRIC_COUNTER_NAMES value must not appear in eventLedgerEventSchema, and the package is now version 2.2.0-rc.5. The sibling test still says the post-rc.38 cutover should flip to zero overlap but asserts expect(overlap.length).toBe(counterNames.size), so the suite stays green while the forbidden dual-write overlap remains and can mask regressions in metric/event separation.
fix_direction: Flip the G11 test to expect(overlap).toEqual([]) for the current v2.2 line, remove metric counter event types from the event ledger schema, and adjust the G8 leak test/implementation to detect raw metric-event rows without depending on those forbidden rows parsing as valid typed ledger events.

context.notes: Discovered by comprehensive testing issue discovery DBP-20260609-013801

issue_history.note: Discovered by comprehensive testing issue discovery DBP-20260609-013801

title: Werewolf snapshot fixture still pins project-local .fabric knowledge as production-shaped test data
description: The werewolf fixture test asserts that a tarred project-local .fabric tree still contains agents.meta.json and .fabric/knowledge, then runs doctor against that layout; the policy file calls the same tarball a realistic production-shaped knowledge base, and the CLI cross-client parity smoke test reuses it. In the store-only era this golden fixture lets doctor/parity tests stay green without exercising mounted store config, store.json, or cross-store read/write resolution, so fixture updates can preserve the retired co-location contract instead of the current user workflow.
fix_direction: Regenerate the dogfood fixture as a mounted-store project: create a store with store.json under an isolated Fabric home, bind it via .fabric/fabric-config.json, place knowledge entries in the store, and assert doctor/parity behavior through the store resolver. If the old tarball is retained, mark it legacy-only and stop using it as the production-shaped baseline.

context.notes: Discovered by comprehensive testing issue discovery DBP-20260609-013801

issue_history.note: Discovered by comprehensive testing issue discovery DBP-20260609-013801

title: Orphan archive-hint snapshot preserves retired plan_context copy with no test owner
description: packages/cli/__tests__/integration/__snapshots__/archive-hint-copy.test.ts.snap still contains archive reminder goldens phrased around accumulated plan_context calls, but rg --files finds no matching archive-hint-copy.test.ts/js owner. Vitest will ignore the orphan snapshot, leaving stale retired-contract output in the repo with no active gate to force either deletion or an update to the current edit-count/fab_recall-era wording.
fix_direction: Delete the orphan snapshot, or restore a real semantic test that asserts the current archive signal wording and source, then add a small orphan-snapshot check so .snap files cannot outlive their owning tests.

context.notes: Discovered by comprehensive testing issue discovery DBP-20260609-013801

issue_history.note: Discovered by comprehensive testing issue discovery DBP-20260609-013801

title: Shared recall perf test gates on real p95 timing
description: The shared store recall perf test measures 30 real iterations with performance.now() and fails if read-set p95 exceeds full-scan p95 * 1.2. The structural read-set behavior is already asserted separately, but this timing assertion can fail from CPU, cache, GC, or filesystem jitter and make the package test gate environment-dependent.
fix_direction: Keep the structural assertions that prove only the read-set is scanned, and move timing to a non-blocking benchmark/report script or use injected counters/instrumentation instead of wall-clock p95 in Vitest.

context.notes: Discovered by comprehensive testing issue discovery DBP-20260609-013801

issue_history.note: Discovered by comprehensive testing issue discovery DBP-20260609-013801

title: HTTP app tests inherit a global FABRIC_PROJECT_ROOT mutation
description: createFabricHttpApp writes process.env.FABRIC_PROJECT_ROOT during app construction, while its dispose path only clears timers/watchers and the integration test afterEach only calls dispose and removes the temp dir. Later tests in the same worker can inherit a deleted temp project root, making behavior order-dependent when code falls back to process.env.FABRIC_PROJECT_ROOT.
fix_direction: Avoid mutating process.env in createFabricHttpApp; pass projectRoot explicitly to session/server creation. If a temporary env bridge is still required, save the prior value and restore it in dispose, and add an integration test that asserts FABRIC_PROJECT_ROOT is restored after dispose.

context.notes: Discovered by comprehensive testing issue discovery DBP-20260609-013801

issue_history.note: Discovered by comprehensive testing issue discovery DBP-20260609-013801

title: config-loader tests create temp fixtures inside the real worktree
description: config-loader.test.ts creates per-test directories under process.cwd() such as .tmp-config-loader-tests using Date.now()/Math.random(), then removes only the current tempDir in afterEach. An interrupted test run leaves untracked fixture directories in the repository, which can pollute later git/worktree scans and local developer state.
fix_direction: Use mkdtemp under os.tmpdir() for these fixtures, or create one tracked suite temp root outside the repository and remove it in afterAll; avoid writing throwaway test data under process.cwd().

context.notes: Discovered by comprehensive testing issue discovery DBP-20260609-013801
